Let's start with the technical side: the game utilizes only about 60-70% of my GPU and around 40-50% of my CPU. Meanwhile, the FPS is all over the place, fluctuating wildly between 80 and 160. For comparison, Red Dead Redemption 2 gives me a stable 80-100 frames with my GPU running at a solid 100%. The optimization here makes zero sense. ​Now, about the game itself. This isn't even a game anymore; it's just a sweatshop for bot farms to grind cases and a cash cow for Steam's owner. Think about it: a single farm running 1,000 bots—where every single one needs a Prime status—brings Valve $15,000 in raw cash just for the buy-in. This inflates the player count, shareholders see "growing numbers" and pump in even more money, and at the end of the day, ordinary players are the only ones suffering in this food chain. ​I exclusively play Deathmatch to warm up, and lately, it's been unplayable. You join a match, and there are like 5 bots just standing afk or instantly leaving. But the bots have become so damn bold that they’ve started kick-voting you the exact second you switch to Spectate mode just to see what they're doing. You are literally kicked instantly! ​Are you guys even aware that these bots exist?! Why isn't there a specific "Bot" option in the report menu?! Look, if you can't ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ban them, at least create a separate map pool for them. Let these scripts compete against each other in their own sandbox and leave real people alone. ​In my wildest dreams, it would be amazing if—when a bot gets kicked—their entire inventory value (either in skins or cash equivalent) was distributed among the real players in that match as a moral compensation.  Начну с технички, игра загружает видеокарту примерно на 60-70 % и процессор около 40-50%. Fps скачет от 80 до 160. Хотя в Read Dead Redemption 2 около 80-100 кадров при 100 % загрузке видеокарты.   Теперь про саму игру, это не игра, это место для фарма кейсов для ботов и фарм денег для владельца стима, так как, допустим, одна ферма на 1000 ботов, каждому из которых, которому требуется prime, приносит стиму 15000$ Повышенный онлайн, ещё больше вложение денег от акционеров, и в итоге из всех этих звеньев страдают только обычные игроки.  Я играю чисто в DM, и бывало, что в катке по 5 ботов, которые поспешно выходят из неё или бездействуют, когда заходишь в режим «наблюдения».  Но боты настолько охренели, что начали выкидывать из катки, как только заходишь в режим «наблюдения». Вы вообще в курсе, что есть боты?! Почему при жалобе нет уточнения, что это «бот»?! Вы, если не можете их банить, создайте отдельную группу карт, и пусть эти боты сами с собой соревнуются. В моих влажных мечтах было бы хорошо, чтобы, когда бота кикали, его весь инвентарь в денежном эквиваленте или скинами распределяли между участниками, с кем он играл, в качестве моральной компенсации.

CS2 is less of a competitive shooter and more of a social experiment to determine how much nonsense a human being can tolerate before uninstalling.    Every other match is a coin flip between obvious cheaters, people screaming slurs into their microphones, and teammates whose entire personality revolves around insulting women the second they hear a female voice. The community somehow manages to combine the maturity of a middle school Xbox lobby with the ego of aspiring esports pros.    You’ll also meet players who haven’t seen daylight in weeks, take every round like it’s the grand final of a major, and blame everyone except themselves after charging mid for the tenth time.    The gunplay is still great, which somehow makes all of this even more frustrating. Beneath the layers of toxicity, misogyny, and questionable anti-cheat effectiveness is a fantastic game desperately trying to survive its own player base.    10/10 gameplay.    2/10 community.

2k+ hours in to this game and 10k+ hours in to this franchise. This entire franchise is being subsidized by the in game gambling; i.e. loot crates & skins market. If the in game skins market wasn't worth millions of dollars, this game would have died 10-15 years ago. The actual gameplay is the same old and the competitive scene is more or less limited to pro leagues that take place on THIRD PARTY platforms like FaceIt. That's how bad the in game anti cheat is in this game, NOBODY that takes this game seriously plays competitively on it. Cheaters and hackers are so prevalent in game that third party platforms have their own market just based on superior anti cheat processes. This game is a sad, hollow shell of what it once was. And the developers know this; they just like making money because at the end of the day you have to buy this game (used to be $9.99) to even play on said third party platforms + millions in a market for in game skins. Very obviously, the developers spend more time and effort in creating new skins than working towards a fix for hackers in game or fixing the illogical matchmaking process. As a new player, you WILL go against more "smurfs" (experienced players that create new accounts or lose on purpose to derank to play in low level ranks to feel better about themselves) and hackers than actual other new players. This is because, as mentioned before, any experienced players that take this game seriously is not playing in game; they are playing on third party platforms like FaceIt. You will never achieve anything more than frustration trying to play this game standalone. Most players are usually in mod servers that aren't even related to the actual game, like zombie mods and zombie escape mods. This franchise used to be a leader in first person shooters, now its just a cash grab for developers trying to get as many people addicted to the in game gambling mechanics related to skins. Even as a free game, not worth your time, believe me. Unless you realllly think you wanna go "pro" for some odd reason or realllly like gambling.

I was once a man with dreams. A man who believed in something. I believed in headshots, i clutch rounds, in the quiet dignity of a well thrown smoke. That man is gone. Valve killed him. Not with a bullet, with a teammate.    Every single match, without fail, as if ordained by some ancient and malevolent god, I am placed with at least one individual who communicates exclusively in a language I do not speak, directed at me, in a tone that suggests I have personally wronged his entire bloodline. He calls me things. Terrible things. I don't know what half of them mean but I know, in my bones, that they are not compliments.    The other teammate is screaming blyat into voice at 200 words per minute while running directly at a wall. He is not playing the game. He is conducting a one man performance piece about the futility of existence, and I am the unwilling audience.    This is not a game. It is an anthropological study of how quickly civilization collapses when you give five strangers guns and no consequences. I can now confirm the answer is four seconds. Four seconds into the buy phase and someone is already screaming something that would get them banned from every country with a functioning judiciary.    Every hour i play haunts me. I dream in kill cams. I wake up sweating, hearing footsteps that aren't there, checking corners in my own kitchen. My family is concerned. I told them it was fine. It is not fine.

Counter-Strike 2 is, in my opinion, one of the most frustrating games I have ever played. Many players believe that cheating is still a serious problem. Because of this, some matches feel unfair and disappointing.    Another issue is that players often complain about bugs, server problems, and matchmaking. Sometimes it feels like skill does not matter as much as it should. When you spend time practicing and then lose to someone who seems suspicious, it can make the experience much less enjoyable.    Despite having great graphics and exciting gameplay, Counter-Strike 2 still has many problems that need to be fixed. If Valve improves the anti-cheat system and matchmaking, the game could become much better. Until then, many players will continue to criticize it and call it one of the most frustrating competitive games available today.    Zemun is LGBTQ

On your team, you either get silent vegetables or screaming ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ who yell into their mics and ♥♥♥♥ all over each other in voice chat instead of playing the game.  And don't get me started on the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ optimization – stutters, crashes, and tickrate that makes your bullets go absolutely nowhere near where you aimed. The only way to play without cheating scum is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Faceit. But that comes with its own pus-filled issues: their janky client, local 'pros' with egos the size of Everest, and you'll still get spam-reported just for landing a headshot through a smoke.  The most ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up part is the player base that crawls into this ♥♥♥♥. On one side — schoolkids with cheats who found some discount software for 3 bucks. They toggle spinbot right in the pistol round, but in voice chat they act all cocky: 'Git gud, old man.' On the other side — dudes in their 30s who should be paying mortgages and taking their kids to kindergarten, but instead they go buy private cheats with their ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ paycheck. Just to boost their Premier rank and feel important somewhere — like measuring ♥♥♥♥♥ with other pathetic old farts. Are you serious, man? You spent your own money on CS hacks instead of actually practicing or just enjoying the game?  And here's a special piece of ♥♥♥♥ — that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Prime upgrade for $15 (or whatever in rubles). I bought it, thinking there'd be clean matches, normal people. But what's the point? ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ NOTHING. The same exact cheaters play on Prime as on free accounts. This garbage gives ZERO advantage — absolutely nothing. You just flushed your money down the toilet. Thanks, Valve.  And on top of this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ circus, the skin market is crashing hard. Prices on knives and gloves are tanking because people are fleeing this dumpster fire. Your inventory that you've been building for years is losing value faster than Bitcoin at rock bottom.  But the worst part isn't even the money. I've spent fifteen hundred hours on this game. Fifteen hundred ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hours of my life. And now I clearly realize: that time was completely wasted. No fun, no growth — just constant frustration, cheaters, toxic shouting, and false hopes about the next patch that'll fix everything. It didn't fix a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ thing.  So in the end, we're witnessing a slow but steady decline. From the legendary CS 1.6, where skill and tactics were respected — to this beta version made by some incompetent devs who probably studied at a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ technical school in a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ neighborhood. A place where basic smokes disappear into the floor and the anticheat works on the principle of 'maybe it'll pass.' Valve, go ♥♥♥♥ yourselves with your 'successor.' This isn't CS. This is a piss-stained parody of itself. I regret every single hour and every single dollar. Don't fall for it like I did. This is a dumpster fire. Burn in hell.

Counter-Strike is, without a doubt, the greatest game of all time for me. I've been playing Counter-Strike for over 20 years, and no other game has managed to keep me coming back for so long.  Over the years, I've met some amazing people through this game. Some of them became close friends, and a few I even met in real life. Very few games can create connections like that.  The gameplay is still fantastic. The movement feels responsive and satisfying, and the hit registration and feedback are usually instant and accurate. It's easy to get into, but incredibly difficult to master, which is one of the reasons why Counter-Strike remains relevant after all these years.  The maps are excellent, ranging from timeless classics to the occasional new and exciting community-made maps that keep things fresh. The graphics and the Source 2 engine are also a clear improvement and provide a modern look without sacrificing the core Counter-Strike experience.  Would I recommend Counter-Strike 2?  Honestly, I would — if it weren't for several major issues.  The cheating problem remains one of the biggest flaws of the game. Even worse, casual modes such as Deathmatch and Arms Race have become infested with bots to the point where they are practically unplayable at times. The overall selection of casual game modes is also surprisingly limited for a game of this size and age.  What makes this especially frustrating is the impression that Valve is either unwilling or unable to meaningfully address the cheating and bot problems. From a player's perspective, there seem to be very few effective measures being taken, while update after update focuses heavily on new skins and cosmetics.  What started as an incredible local multiplayer experience during my youth now feels like it's slowly crashing into the ground. Meanwhile, players are expected to get excited about the next case, sticker, or weapon finish.  Another thing that has changed for me is the whole case-opening experience. It simply doesn't feel rewarding anymore. What used to be an exciting little bonus has become something I barely care about nowadays. Opening cases no longer gives me the sense of anticipation or satisfaction it once did, making the constant focus on skins and cosmetics feel even more disconnected from the issues that actually affect the gameplay experience.  In the end, Counter-Strike 2 is still an amazing game at its core. The foundation is as strong as ever, and I will continue to play it. However, in its current state, I simply cannot recommend it to new players.  **Great game. Poorly managed problems.**

CS2 in 2026 is an absolute joke. It’s infuriating that we’re still dealing with the same fundamental issues that have plagued this game since launch.    Let’s talk about the elephant in the room: the cheaters. It is frankly pathetic that a multi-billion dollar company like Valve can’t implement an anti-cheat that actually works. If FACEIT can maintain a system that catches 95% of these scumbags, there is zero excuse for VAC to be this useless. Every other lobby in Premier is a coin flip on whether you’re playing a game or watching a spinbotter destroy the lobby. It’s killing the competitive integrity of the game.    Then there’s the "sub-tick" system. Valve keeps trying to sell this as "better than 128-tick," but it feels like hot garbage. Bullet registration is consistently inconsistent. You’ll have moments where you’re dead on target, spray control is perfect, and nothing hits. The desync is real, the movement feels heavy compared to CS:GO, and the overall "snappiness" that made CS a legend is just gone.     Valve is clearly more interested in pushing out skin updates and low-effort patches than actually addressing the core technical debt and the rampant cheating problem. They’ve turned a masterpiece into a chore. If you want to play a competitive FPS, go somewhere else until Valve decides they actually give a damn. Verdict: 2/10. Play it only if you hate yourself.

Title: "What you see is what you get"... if what you want to see is absolute chaos.  Valve promised us the future of tactical shooters, but instead, they deleted CS:GO and handed us a broken tech demo. ​Let’s talk about the "revolutionary" sub-tick system. It’s amazing! I can now peek, run 3 meters behind a solid concrete wall, check my emails, and THEN die to a headshot from someone who didn't even see me on their screen. True magic. ​If you enjoy playing against spin-bots, flying accounts, and wizards who pre-fire you from across the map through three walls, Premier mode is for you. VAC doesn't stand for Valve Anti-Cheat anymore; it stands for Valve Allows Cheaters. The only thing getting banned in this game is my sanity. ​Optimization is a whole different joke. My PC used to run CS:GO at a smooth 300+ FPS. Now, every time a smoke grenade pops, my graphics card sounds like a SpaceX rocket taking off, and my FPS drops to PowerPoint presentation levels. The rubber-banding and random crashes aren't bugs; at this point, they are core gameplay features. ​But hey, at least the weapon finishes look shiny while the game is dying, right? ​Pros: ​Opened my eyes to how peaceful real life is when I uninstall it. ​Cons: ​Everything else. ​Rating: 0/10. Gabe, please sell the IP to a company that actually plays their own game.

0/10 Experience    Every Competitive and Premier match feels like a gamble on who gets the most wall hackers. Players somehow know exactly where everyone is at all times, perfectly timing peeks and pre-fires with zero information. Instead of focusing on strategy and aim, you're left wondering whether you're playing against actual players or people using cheats.    CS2 has great mechanics, but none of that matters when the integrity of matches feels completely broken. The anti-cheat system needs major improvements because right now fair games feel like the exception, not the rule. Save yourself the frustration.

CS2 has become frustrating to play because it feels like there are wall hackers in almost every match, whether it's Competitive or Premier. Too many players seem to have impossible awareness, constantly pre-firing angles and tracking people through walls. The anti-cheat system doesn't feel effective enough, and legitimate players are left questioning every game.    The core gameplay is still good, but it's hard to enjoy when fair matches are so rare. Until cheating is addressed more aggressively, it's difficult to recommend spending serious time on CS2. ⭐ 2/10
